items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I finally got the job at the museum.

options.A: Well, that's the news I was hoping for.
options.B: The museum has a new dinosaur exhibit.
options.C: I lost my job there last spring.
options.D: Where did you leave your application?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词"Well"开头，间接地表达了为朋友感到高兴和欣慰，是对好消息的自然回应。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"museum"扯到展览，没有回应这个好消息。C跑题，谈的是说话人自己丢工作的过去事件，情感和情境都不匹配。D答非所问，对方分享的是已经得到工作的结果，却问申请放在哪里，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: How can I get your notes from today's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was completely full today.
options.B: Sure, I'll email them to you tonight.
options.C: I take notes on my laptop now.
options.D: When does the next lecture start?

items.explanation: 正确答案B以"Sure"开头，间接答应并说明具体方式（晚上发邮件），自然推进对话。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"lecture"描述讲堂很满，没有回应借笔记的请求。C跑题，只说自己用笔记本电脑记笔记，并没有回答怎么把笔记给对方。D答非所问，对方问怎么拿到笔记，却回答下节课什么时候开始，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: What's the fastest way to reach the north lot?

options.A: The lot was just repaved over the weekend.
options.B: Yes, parking permits are required there.
options.C: Your best bet is the shuttle out front.
options.D: I parked there once last semester.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用了习语"Your best bet is..."（你最好的选择是），自然地给出最快的方式——坐门口的班车。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"lot"说停车场刚重铺，没回答怎么去。B答非所问，对方问"怎么去"却用"Yes"回答许可证问题，属于错误问题类型。D跑题，只说自己上学期在那停过车，没有提供到达方式。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Can someone here look over my essay draft?

options.A: I wrote three essays last term.
options.B: The center moved to the second floor.
options.C: Are you taking the writing seminar?
options.D: Yes, a tutor can read it with you now.

items.explanation: 正确答案D直接回答了问题——是的，现在有辅导员可以陪你看草稿。A跑题，只谈自己上学期写了三篇文章，与当下求助无关。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"center"说写作中心搬到二楼，没回答能不能帮看草稿。C答非所问，对方问能否帮忙看，却反问是否在上写作研讨课，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: The spring concert is happening this Friday.

options.A: Actually, I already bought us two tickets.
options.B: The concert hall seats about a thousand.
options.C: I missed last year's spring festival.
options.D: Where do you usually buy your groceries?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接回应说自己已经买好两张票，自然推进对话并暗示一起去。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"concert"说音乐厅能坐多少人，没回应这个消息。C跑题，谈的是自己错过去年春季活动的过去事件。D完全跑题，问对方平时在哪买杂货，与音乐会毫无关系。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you have the second edition of this textbook?

options.A: I read that textbook over the summer.
options.B: Would the third edition work for you instead?
options.C: The reading room closes at eight tonight.
options.D: Yes, I know several students using it.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用反问推进对话，提出第三版是否也可以，是图书馆员处理"有没有某版本"的自然方式。A跑题，只说自己暑假读过这本书，没回答馆藏问题。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住图书馆情境说阅览室八点关门，与版本无关。D是审计中常见的歧义陷阱类型——用"know"的"认识"义（认识几个用书的学生），并没有真正回答是否有这一版，属于多义/误读陷阱。

items.speaker: Where can I find your weekend opening hours?

options.A: The weekend crowds are usually pretty heavy.
options.B: Yes, we open quite early on Saturdays.
options.C: Don't worry, we're open all weekend long.
options.D: The store moved to a new location downtown.

items.explanation: 正确答案C是情境转移——不纠结于"在哪查"，而是直接解决背后的真实需求（用"Don't worry"安抚并告知周末全天营业）。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"weekend"说周末人很多，没解决需求。B答非所问，对方问"在哪查营业时间"，却用"Yes"回答，属于错误问题类型。D跑题，说店搬到市中心新址，与营业时间无关。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Why is there banging coming from upstairs?

options.A: The upstairs unit has a lovely balcony.
options.B: Banging on doors is rude, I agree.
options.C: When did you move into this building?
options.D: I'll go ask them to keep it down.

items.explanation: 正确答案D是情境转移——不解释为什么有声音，而是直接解决背后的困扰（我去让他们小声点）。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"upstairs"夸楼上阳台漂亮，无视困扰。B误读了意图，把"banging"理解成敲门并空泛附和，没有解决问题。C答非所问，对方问声音原因，却反问你什么时候搬进来的，属于错误问题类型。
